author: Anne
content: People think this is common knowledge but it isn't. Teachers are constantly promoting usage of digital technology in schools because they are incompetent at teaching and they cover up the fact by saying technology is a human right and nonsense like that. I do admit that technology did help during the time of covid 19, but I ask myself constantly whether it was actually necessary to continue studying at the same pace. Wouldn't it have been better to make children retake their classes after the schools opened? An year or two of delay in their education wouldn't have been that disastrous... Delayed education would be more preferable to inefficient education, especially among children younger than 15. It just showcases the greed and apathy our society shows to distressed people. Nobody cared whether the youth actually learnt anything, as long as the schools and colleges were run and everyone got their paycheck. Children these days can't physically write with a pen/pencil on paper. Relying so much on technology for teaching during covid 19 made teachers so accustomed to the practice that they still insist on doing so after schools reopened. We cannot excuse this. Many teachers today are taking advantage of technology to laze off and aren't doing what they're paid to do. author: Klara
content: I would say you can shop anywhere - even Abercrombie (never thought I’d say that!). I just returned two pairs of jeans (curve love) at Abercrombie but did order two more. There is no branding on them which I like and they make curvy jeans - as someone who is 5’4 and 34-24-36 - I appreciate the options they offer. Not sure if the other two pair I ordered will work - they might not - but they don’t look too young. And if I don’t like them fine but I do think there are some classy options. I think as long as you keep it clean - simple and classy it doesn’t matter where you buy your clothes. It’s so hard to find stores for over 40 that don’t look too frumpy and I have issues with finding clothes that fit me. So I am opening up my options…with caution lol. Rather than saying where not to go - maybe offer some places that are suitable. Everlane I think is good too. Madewell - again with caution as it tends to be the 25-35. White House black market. I never have good luck at macys it honestly confuses me where to go there. It seems like it’s juniors or 60 somethings? Banana republic - good but as I’ve gotten older I don’t love everything like I used to. I think the type of clothes you get is more important than the brand. I’d say no big logos. Or any logos. Nothing too tight. Layers. I’ll wear a tank with jeans (not distressed) but I’ll tuck it in and wear a boyfriend button up - unbuttoned….and maybe a low heel or toms if it’s a casual spring or summer. But really - I’m learning as I get older - wear what you want and don’t be so judgy. For myself I try to keep it toned down and classy - comfortable - and covered up. But I do struggle still. Some clothes seem too…stuffy…but I don’t want to look like I’m trying to dress like I’m 25 or even 30.

I would say just make sure they are clean and google some home remedies to keep your nails looking white. Maybe grow them out a little but not too long, or trim, keep them clean and they'll get lighter at the tips and you can use "hard as nails" as it leaves a gloss on them but also helps with growth and keeping them healthy and thick. Also there's some other things you can focus on like using a lip balm/vaseline or lip gloss on your lips, tinted moisturizer, etc. There's a ton of things you can do to focus on the other parts of your body instead! But you're beautiful either way, regardless if you do nails/eyebrows or not! Hope these tips help!

author: Eliana
content: Hey ladies! Okay so this happened a while back, but I still can't seem to move on over it, lol. Basically there's this guy I low key had a crush on... We'll call him Bob. Well anyway Bob and I low key knew each other from where he worked. I was a customer, he worked there, but we ALWAYS talked whether it was how our day was going, school, etc. And actually we were both homeschooled and so we had that in common. One day, we were both talking and it was a good conversation about homeschool, college, etc and so I gave him my number. I said "here's my number, if you ever want to talk later" and OMG I was literally so nervous and embarrased I ended up nearly running, lol! But he took it and looked at me still smiling (looking back now I think he was a little shocked) but he's a nice guy and I respect how he held it with care and held eye contact. I was so in love for the rest of the day and wouldn't stop checking my phone. I was feeling of proud of myself because usually I've always had low self confidence and this was the first time I'd ever gotten out there and done something like this. And I ended up liking him a lot. But long story short... he never called me. He ended up running away/ avoiding me the last two times I saw him, I was going to try to apologize but he nearly does everything he can to stay away. It mad me REALLY sad before, it felt like I was going through a little breakup, lol. Fast forward to now, he doesn't avoid me anymore but he does talk to other girls, like a lot, and keep in mind I have curly hair and am more "plus sized" the girls he talks to have straight hair, so I kind of think its the appearance thing? I love myself for who I am and I still feel confident, but it just makes me sad this little "rejection" thing and at the same time I sometimes kind of feel like a terrible person thinking I overstepped and made him feel uncomfortable. Any tips or advice????

author: ATia
content: I think the 14 year old that took offense to the article, is exactly proving the article right and i think its too funny and ironic You may be mature, and watch the news ..lol.. but that's the thing, maturity is an ongoing process, and we shouldn't think we have it figured out, even adults are still learning, and a lot of teenagers get to a certain stage where they feel they are mature, and have no need to learn anything more, they have arrived, and take any level of constructive criticism as chastisement. If the article does not apply to you then I do not understand the need to comment in defense of oneself. However there ARE parents out there at their wits end feeling as though they have teenagers that hate them by way of their treatment towards said parent. Which brings me to my other point of contention with your comment, did you actually read every point made in this article, because I did and I didn't see anywhere in the article it referred to teenagers as "slouchy" , "lazy" or "people who don't think about the future" to me the article helped to see a different point of view than myself.... the point of view of a teenager, the fact that teenagers deal with so much, school work, home work, house work, social pressures, needing to fit in, wanting what they cannot have, wanting to be accepted while maintaining a level of individualism, it can be hard, and here us parents come along and make sometimes unreasonable demands on our teens. I am learning that teenage brains are different, that teenagers need more sleep than most ages, that teenagers do better in schools that start later, etc etc.. and throw in hormones and social pressure and now I realize the anger isn't really anger but rather frustration maybe even resentment, because they feel they are not being heard or understood. I have to constantly remind myself of how i felt when an adult made stupid comments about my music, hair, clothing, friends, my generation in general and tell myself. Just because you do not get it doesn't make it wrong or strange. anyhoo thanks for reading this novella

author: Maya
content: I never liked my birth name, and never will. Before I was born, my parents were thinking on giving me two names, one of which being Elizabeth, and in my personal preference I like it so much better than what they ended up naming me. They ended up only giving me one name because it would’ve been too long with my last names. I was named after a Jewish prophet because my dad’s a Jewish, but it’s not even one of the 7 prophets, not even close, and there’s many other great Jewish names I could’ve been named as, such as Maya (what I want to be referred as). I never liked my real name for a number of reasons, such as of how rare it is. Sure, it’s a pretty common name in Israel but finding things such as famous actors, movie or cartoon characters who have my name is a task, and when you find one it’s so obscure you don’t even know what they’re from. I’ve always liked the idea of a character being named just like you, even if it is for the better or for the worse (unless you’re named something like Adolf, in that case…. I’m real sorry). Another thing I hate about my name is the pronunciation in English. I’m making plans to move to the USA, and when that time comes I want people to greet me with a name that’s easy to pronounce. Even though I’ve mentioned my dad being Jewish and choosing a name from Israel, I’m actually Mexican, so pronouncing my name in Spanish isn’t very difficult, but in English, it just sounds like the word “jail” and I don’t want people to know me as “jail”. Another thing is, despite being a gender neutral name, and despite it being more female oriented in Israel, most people make it a male oriented name, with only one letter apart from being a completely male name so whenever I tell people my real name I get told “oh that’s a boys name” and that makes me pretty insecure. I also don’t really like shortening my last names since they’re kinda weird too. Instead of changing my first name, I’ll add “Maya” as a middle name, so when I get to know other people they have less difficulty pronouncing my name right and I get more comfortable with my identity, while still having my family members call me my real birth name.
